feature: LINE webhook signature verification and event dispatch
user_story: As the LINE platform, I want to deliver webhook events to NBHD United so that the system can react to user activity in real-time.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/line/webhook/ — CSRF exempt. First validates the X-Line-Signature HMAC-SHA256 against LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET; missing or invalid signature returns HTTP 403. Parses JSON body; invalid JSON returns HTTP 400. For each event in the events array, claims the webhookEventId in ProcessedInboundEvent (line:<webhookEventId>) before any side effect — duplicate events return immediately (idempotent). Each event is dispatched in a daemon thread (except in test mode where NBHD_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_THREADS=True runs inline). Sets RLS service-role context on each thread. Routes to _handle_message, _handle_follow, _handle_unfollow, or _handle_postback; unknown types are logged and ignored. Always returns HTTP 200 to LINE within 1 second.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API (signature source), Supabase/Postgres (ProcessedInboundEvent dedup ledger)
inventory_notes: LINE re-delivers events (deliveryContext.isRedelivery=true) when 200 is slow. The dedup gate is the only protection against double-processing. Threads are daemon so they don't prevent process shutdown but also can't outlive the process.

user_story: As a LINE user who adds the bot, I want to receive a welcome message so that I know how to connect my account.
expected_behaviour: Triggered by LINE follow event. Resolves the LINE user ID against User.line_user_id. If already linked: sends a branded short bubble Flex message welcoming back the subscriber by display name. If unlinked: sends a full branded Flex bubble instructing the user to sign up at FRONTEND_URL and connect LINE from Settings. Both paths use _send_line_flex (Push API, no reply token). No HTTP response — runs asynchronously in a daemon thread.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API Push (LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN), Supabase/Postgres (User model lookup)

user_story: As a LINE user who blocks or unfollows the bot, I want my LINE account automatically unlinked so that stale IDs don't cause delivery errors.
expected_behaviour: Triggered by LINE unfollow event. Looks up User by line_user_id. Clears line_user_id and line_display_name. If preferred_channel was 'line', resets it to 'telegram'. Saves with update_fields. If user is not found (unlinked user), silently returns. No message is sent back (the user has blocked the bot). Side effect: proactive cron delivery will fall back to Telegram (or fail with no_channel_linked) on subsequent sends.
inventory_notes: preferred_channel falls back to 'telegram' even if Telegram is not linked — the user may need to reconnect.

feature: LINE text message ingest and forwarding pipeline
user_story: As a LINE subscriber, I want to send text messages to the bot so that my AI assistant can respond to my questions.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with type=text. Strips and validates text. Detects link_ prefix and routes to account-linking. Resolves tenant via line_user_id; sends a warning Flex if unrecognized. Checks tenant status: PENDING/PROVISIONING sends a 'waking up' bubble; SUSPENDED without active subscription sends billing URL. Calls check_budget — on budget exhaustion, hibernates tenant and sends error message. Calls handle_hibernated_message for hibernated containers: buffers the message, triggers wake, returns ACK_FRESH (sends 'waking' bubble), ACK_RECONNECT ('reconnecting' bubble), or SILENT (no ack). For onboarding: gates non-text during onboarding, calls get_onboarding_response. For live tenants: shows loading animation, updates last_message_at, extracts quote-reply context (LineOutboundMessage lookup), prepends proactive context + datetime marker + chat marker, then calls enqueue_message_for_tenant to place the message in PendingMessage. Drain is handled asynchronously by QStash.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (User/Tenant/PendingMessage/LineOutboundMessage/ProactiveOutbound), LINE Messaging API Push, OpenClaw container (via drain task), QStash (drain task dispatch), apps/billing (check_budget), apps/orchestrator (wake_hibernated_tenant)
inventory_notes: reply_token is captured in PendingMessage payload but NOT used at drain time — by then the 1-minute Reply API window has typically expired. Drain always uses Push API. Session key is flat (line_user_id) — no workspace routing since PR removing it.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber, I want to send voice messages and have them transcribed so that my AI assistant can understand spoken input.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with type=audio. Downloads audio binary from LINE Content API (https://api-data.line.me/v2/bot/message/<id>/content) using LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN. Detects extension from content-type header (m4a/ogg/wav, default m4a). POSTs to OpenAI Whisper API (whisper-1 model) with OPENAI_API_KEY. On success: prepends '🎤 Voice message: "<transcript>"' and processes as a text message. On failure: sends a warning status bubble. Shows loading animation before transcription and again after (Whisper may consume the first 60s window). VOICE_CHAT_TIMEOUT_EXTRA = 60 extra seconds is defined but the drain timeout is resolved separately in _resolve_chat_timeout.
dependencies: LINE Content API (audio download), OpenAI Whisper API (transcription, requires OPENAI_API_KEY), LINE Messaging API Push (response delivery)
inventory_notes: Falls back gracefully — on transcription failure, sends a warning Flex bubble rather than silently dropping the message. OPENAI_API_KEY is required; if absent, immediately fails with a warning log.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber, I want to send stickers and have my assistant respond naturally so that I can communicate emotionally without typing.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with type=sticker. Extracts keywords and stickerResourceType from the event. If keywords are present (up to 5), builds a framed directive '[User sent a LINE sticker expressing: <keywords>. Respond naturally…]'. If no keywords, uses a generic '[User sent a LINE sticker (no keywords available)…]'. This synthetic text is then processed exactly like a text message through the standard pipeline (budget check, queue, container forward). Not allowed during onboarding steps — sends a 'stickers not ready yet' short bubble.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingMessage), OpenClaw container (via drain), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: LINE sticker keywords are LINE-platform-generated emotional tags, not OCR; they vary by sticker pack. Emoji bullets and tone are agent-driven.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber who sends an image, video, or location, I want to receive a clear error message so that I know those types are not supported.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with types other than text, audio, or sticker (e.g. image, video, location, file). Sends a warning status Flex bubble 'I can process text, voice, and stickers. Please send one of those!' via Push API. Returns without enqueueing anything.

feature: LINE account linking via deep link token
user_story: As a subscriber who scanned the QR code or tapped the LINE deep link, I want my LINE account to be linked to my NBHD United account so that I can chat with my assistant on LINE.
expected_behaviour: Triggered when an inbound LINE text message starts with 'link_'. Strips the 'link_' prefix to get the token. Fetches the LINE display name from the LINE Profile API (GET /v2/bot/profile/<userId>). Calls process_line_link_token: looks up LineLinkToken by token value, returns error if not found or is_valid is False (expired or used). Checks for collision (same LINE user linked to a different NBHD user) — returns error if so. Sets User.line_user_id and line_display_name (updates display_name from 'Friend' if not yet personalized). Marks the token used=True. If the tenant's container is ACTIVE, publishes 'update_tenant_config' via QStash to push the updated config to the container. Sends a plain text success message '✅ Welcome, <name>! Your LINE account is now connected.' Tokens expire after 15 minutes.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (LineLinkToken, User model), LINE Profile API (display name), QStash (update_tenant_config task), OpenClaw container (config update)
inventory_notes: One-time token, 15-minute TTL. If the LINE Profile API fails, linking still succeeds with an empty display name. If another NBHD user already has the same LINE user ID linked, linking is rejected.

user_story: As a new LINE subscriber going through onboarding, I want to tap timezone buttons so that I can configure my assistant's timezone without typing.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE postback events where data starts with 'tz_'. Resolves tenant by line_user_id. Calls handle_onboarding_callback from apps.router.onboarding. Returns an OnboardingReply with text and optional keyboard. Renders via _send_onboarding_reply: builds a short_bubble Flex, attaches Quick Reply buttons (converted from Telegram keyboard format via telegram_keyboard_to_quick_reply), pushes via LINE Push API. Postback data formats: 'tz_country:<code>' and 'tz_zone:<zone>'.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Tenant model update), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: LINE has no language_code in webhook events, so the onboarding always asks the language question (unlike Telegram which can infer from update.message.from.language_code).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve or deny pending AI-proposed actions directly from LINE so that I control what the assistant executes.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE postback events with data starting 'gate_approve:' or 'gate_deny:'. Parses action_id from data. Fetches PendingAction scoped to the tenant. If action not in PENDING status, returns without effect. If is_expired, sets status=EXPIRED, logs ActionAuditLog, calls update_gate_message. Otherwise sets status=APPROVED or DENIED, records responded_at, creates ActionAuditLog, calls update_gate_message. Errors (action not found, parse failure) are logged. No response message is sent to the user — update_gate_message handles the LINE-side update.
inventory_notes: Only PENDING actions can be acted upon. Expired actions are auto-closed on first postback. No idempotency guard beyond the status check.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve or dismiss AI-extracted lessons, goals, and tasks via LINE buttons so that I control what gets saved to my constellation.
expected_behaviour: Handles postback data format 'extract:<action>:<pending_id>'. Actions: approve_lesson, approve_goal, approve_task, undo_lesson, undo_goal, undo_task, dismiss. Fetches PendingExtraction scoped to tenant. For undo actions: checks status is APPROVED (not UNDONE), calls _undo_lesson/_undo_goal/_undo_task, sets status=UNDONE, sends a warning status bubble with 'Removed: <text>'. For approve actions: calls _approve_lesson/_approve_goal/_approve_task, sets status=APPROVED, sends a success status bubble. For dismiss: sets status=DISMISSED, sends warning bubble. Already-resolved items send a friendly 'already added/skipped' text follow-up. If pending_id not found, returns silently.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingExtraction, Lesson, Document models), apps/journal, apps/lessons
inventory_notes: Unlike Telegram path (which uses _answer_callback for immediate ack), LINE postbacks send status bubbles as new Push messages.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to undo a task action applied by the nightly extraction via LINE so that I can correct unwanted changes.
expected_behaviour: Handles postback data format 'task_action:undo:<action_id>'. Delegates to handle_task_action_postback_line from apps.router.task_action_callbacks. Returns (ok, message) tuple. Sends a status bubble with tone='success' or 'warning' based on the result.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingTaskAction or similar), LINE Messaging API Push

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve or dismiss AI-suggested lessons via LINE buttons so that I control what is saved to my constellation.
expected_behaviour: Handles postback data format 'lesson:<action>:<lesson_id>'. Fetches Lesson scoped to tenant where status='pending'. If not found, returns silently. Rehydrates PII for the display text. On 'approve': sets status='approved', sets approved_at, saves, calls process_approved_lesson, sends '✅ Approved: <text[:100]>' via _send_line_follow_up. On 'dismiss': sets status='dismissed', saves, sends '❌ Dismissed: <text[:100]>'. process_approved_lesson errors are logged but don't block the approve response.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Lesson model), apps/lessons (process_approved_lesson), apps/pii (rehydrate_for_tenant), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: Only pending lessons can be acted on. Non-pending lessons are silently ignored (no 'already handled' message).

feature: LINE postback — generic button forward to agent
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to tap Quick Reply buttons attached to assistant messages so that I can respond to the assistant with a single tap.
expected_behaviour: Handles any postback event not matched by tz_/gate_/extract_/task_action_/lesson_ prefixes. Resolves tenant. Calls _forward_to_container with the synthetic text '[User tapped button: "<data>"]'. This goes through the full pipeline: enqueued in PendingMessage, drained to OpenClaw, reply delivered via LINE Push. The postback data (callback_data) is used as the button identifier.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingMessage, Tenant), OpenClaw container (via drain), QStash (drain task)
inventory_notes: This is the fallback for all [[button:label|data]] markers the agent emits. LINE label is capped at 20 chars; data is uncapped in the payload.

feature: LINE pending queue drain — deliver LINE batch to container
user_story: As the system, I want to serialize and deliver LINE messages to the OpenClaw container so that messages arrive in order without overlapping concurrent turns.
expected_behaviour: QStash task drain_pending_messages_for_tenant_task with channel='line'. Claims the oldest PENDING PendingMessage for (tenant, 'line', line_user_id) key using SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Takes a soft lease (delivery_in_flight_until = now + timeout). Builds chat content: singleton batch uses payload.message_text directly; coalesced batch (cold-start) reformats with indexed user texts. POSTs to https://<container_fqdn>/v1/chat/completions with model='openclaw', headers X-Line-User-Id and X-Channel: line. If OpenRouter credit limit detected: hibernates tenant, sends budget-exhausted LINE text, returns False. On success: calls relay_ai_response_to_line to deliver formatted reply, calls _capture_conversation_turn to persist ConversationTurn, records usage. On failure after max attempts: marks status=FAILED, calls _send_apology_for_stale_pending_message via LINE Push. If more pending messages remain, reschedules itself via QStash.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingMessage, ConversationTurn), OpenClaw container (POST /v1/chat/completions), LINE Messaging API Push (relay_ai_response_to_line), QStash (reschedule), apps/billing (record_usage)
inventory_notes: reply_token is NOT used even if present in the payload — the Reply API window is typically closed by drain time. Coalescing is only triggered on cold-start when multiple messages accumulate during container wake. SKIP LOCKED prevents two concurrent drain tasks from processing the same row.

feature: LINE AI response relay — Flex message rendering and delivery
user_story: As the system, I want to format and deliver AI assistant responses to LINE using branded Flex messages so that the user receives a polished, readable reply.
expected_behaviour: relay_ai_response_to_line is the single delivery chokepoint for all LINE AI replies (both live webhook drain and hibernation buffered-delivery path). Rehydrates PII placeholders via pii.redactor. Logs ASCII chart leaks. Extracts [[insight:slug]] markers via insights.markers. Renders [[chart:type]] markers to PNG images uploaded to Azure File Share, building image-type LINE messages with chart URLs. Strips MEDIA: markers (not supported on LINE). Extracts [[button:label|data]] quick reply patterns. Converts markdown tables to key:value plain text. Strips code blocks. Builds Flex bubble via build_flex_bubble (short_bubble, structured bubble, or plain_text fallback based on content classification). Prepends chart image messages. Attaches quick reply to last message. Truncates to max 5 messages (LINE Push limit). Falls back to plain text with _strip_markdown on Flex build failure. Emergency plain text fallback on any exception. Sends via _send_line_messages (Reply API preferred, Push fallback); records sentMessages IDs in LineOutboundMessage for future quote-reply resolution.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API Reply + Push APIs, Azure File Share (chart images), apps/pii (rehydrate_text), apps/insights (extract_and_record_insights), apps/router/charts.py (render_chart), Supabase/Postgres (LineOutboundMessage)
inventory_notes: Three-layer fallback: Flex → plain markdown-stripped text → emergency stripped text on exception. Reply API is tried first (free quota); Push is the fallback. LineOutboundMessage is written best-effort (errors logged, never block delivery).

user_story: As the system, I want to render structured AI responses as branded LINE Flex messages so that subscribers receive a polished, mobile-optimized reading experience.
expected_behaviour: build_flex_bubble classifies content via classify_content: 'short' (<200 chars, no newlines, or <=2000 chars) → build_short_bubble (compact accent-bar bubble); 'structured' (has markdown headers, 3+ bullet items, emoji headers, or 4+ double-newline paragraphs >500 chars) → full branded bubble with sections, header block (first section title becomes colored header), bullet-list components with depth-aware indentation, tappable link components; 'plain_text' (>2000 chars, unstructured) → plain text path. build_status_bubble renders success/error/warning tones with icon+colored background. All bubbles have a 'Tap to copy' clipboard footer with up to 5000 chars. Inline markdown is stripped from all Flex text components. Max 30 body components per bubble (LINE ~50KB limit guard). Carousel builder (build_flex_carousel) supports up to 12 bubbles with optional postback action buttons.
inventory_notes: Clipboard action text is capped at 5000 chars. Flex bubble altText (screen reader / notification preview) is capped at 400 chars. The 30-component guard prevents LINE API 400s for large responses.

feature: LINE quick reply extraction and attachment
user_story: As the system, I want to extract [[button:label|data]] markers from AI responses and attach them as LINE Quick Reply buttons so that subscribers can respond with a single tap.
expected_behaviour: extract_quick_reply_buttons strips [[button:label|data]] patterns from the response text and returns cleaned text plus a list of Quick Reply action items. Labels are capped at 20 chars, data is uncapped. Max 13 items (LINE limit). attach_quick_reply adds quickReply.items to any LINE message object. telegram_keyboard_to_quick_reply converts Telegram inline keyboard format (2D array with text+callback_data) to LINE Quick Reply flat list — used during onboarding so the same OnboardingReply works on both channels.
inventory_notes: Quick reply items trigger postback events; the _handle_generic_postback path forwards them to the agent as '[User tapped button: "<data>"]'.
